title: Idle Resource Identification by Account
spl:
index=summary sourcetype="cloud:idle_candidates" earliest=-1d
| stats sum(estimated_monthly_savings) as idle_dollars by lineItem_UsageAccountId
| lookup aws_account_owner account_id AS lineItem_UsageAccountId OUTPUT owner_email
| sort -idle_dollars
| head 25
description: Rolls up idle candidates (UC-20.1.4) to **account and owner** for FinOps accountability ‚Äî same theme, executive view.
value: Rolls up idle candidates (UC-20.1.4) to **account and owner** for FinOps accountability ‚Äî same theme, executive view.
implementation: Populate `cloud:idle_candidates` from scheduled UC-20.1.4 logic. Monthly email to top account owners.
visualization: Bar chart (idle $ by account), Table (owner, idle $), Single value (fleet idle $).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Cloud metrics, CUR, CMDB.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: Idle detection output, `lineItem_UsageAccountId`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Populate `cloud:idle_candidates` from scheduled UC-20.1.4 logic. Monthly email to top account owners.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=summary sourcetype="cloud:idle_candidates" earliest=-1d
| stats sum(estimated_monthly_savings) as idle_dollars by lineItem_UsageAccountId
| lookup aws_account_owner account_id AS lineItem_UsageAccountId OUTPUT owner_email
| sort -idle_dollars
| head 25
```

Understanding this SPL

**Idle Resource Identification by Account** ‚Äî Rolls up idle candidates (UC-20.1.4) to **account and owner** for FinOps accountability ‚Äî same theme, executive view.

Documented **Data sources**: Idle detection output, `lineItem_UsageAccountId`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Cloud metrics, CUR, CMDB. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: summary; **sourcetype**: cloud:idle_candidates. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=summary, sourcetype="cloud:idle_candidates", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by lineItem_UsageAccountId** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚Ä¢ Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
‚Ä¢ Limits the number of rows with `head`.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
compare with Cost Explorer; compare with the cloud billing console for the same scope, accounts, and time range.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (idle $ by account), Table (owner, idle $), Single value (fleet idle $).
grandmaExplanation: Cost and capacity tools watch how much we spend on cloud and the data center, and where we have spare or strained capacity.
knownFalsePositives: Idle resources for DR standby capacity, scheduled batch processors, or planned future-use staging.
